data.title: U.S. Space Force Signs $4.16 Billion Contract with SpaceX for Satellite-Based Targeting Project
data.description: The U.S. Space Force has entered into a contract with SpaceX valued at $4.16 billion to develop a satellite-based airborne mobile target indication project. According to Jin10, this initiative aims to enhance the capabilities of the U.S. Space Force in tracking and targeting mobile threats from space. The collaboration with SpaceX is expected to leverage advanced satellite technology to improve the precision and effectiveness of military operations. This contract marks a significant step in the ongoing efforts to integrate cutting-edge space technology into national defense strategies.

data.title: Sui Blockchain Experiences Temporary Halt Due to Software Interaction Issues
data.description: Sui's layer-1 blockchain experienced a temporary halt in block production for over three hours and 30 minutes on Friday. According to NS3.AI, the Sui team attributed the disruptions on both Friday and Thursday to the interaction between the 1.72 software release and the address balances and gas charging logic. The team has announced that a long-term software fix has been implemented by a majority of validators to address these issues.

data.title: Bitcoin Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: Bitcoin's price fluctuations could lead to substantial liquidations on major centralized exchanges (CEX), according to data from Coinglass. If Bitcoin falls below $70,137, the liquidation intensity for long positions could reach $1.365 billion. Conversely, if Bitcoin surpasses $77,235, the liquidation intensity for short positions could amount to $1.237 billion.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 640 USDT with a 0.27% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On May 29, 2026, 18:52 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 640 USDT and is now trading at 639.98999 USDT, with a narrowed 0.27% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Jamie Dimon Criticizes Clarity Act and Stablecoin Rewards
data.description: Jamie Dimon, CEO of JPMorgan, announced that his bank, along with others, intends to oppose the Clarity Act in its current form. According to NS3.AI, Dimon expressed criticism towards stablecoin rewards and accused Brian Armstrong of spending significant amounts in Washington to support the legislation.

data.title: U.S. Military Warns of Action in Strait of Hormuz Against Mine-Laying Vessels
data.description: The U.S. Central Command has issued a warning about impending military operations near the Strait of Hormuz, targeting vessels involved in or supporting mine-laying activities under the guise of self-defense. According to ChainCatcher, the U.S. military has accused Iran of attempting to disrupt mine-clearing operations and safe navigation in the Strait. Any vessel found participating in mine-laying activities may be targeted. As of May 29, the U.S. military has redirected 115 commercial ships from their original courses.

data.title: Bank of England Governor Bailey Warns of Uncertainty Despite Potential Ceasefire
data.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey has cautioned that even if a 60-day ceasefire is achieved in the conflict between the U.S., Israel, and Iran, uncertainty will persist, and it will not immediately pave the way for interest rate cuts. According to Jin10, Bailey stated that an agreement nearing completion does not alter his fundamental assessment of the risks this conflict poses to the UK economy. U.S. officials had previously indicated that the agreement was close to being finalized. Bailey remarked, "A 60-day ceasefire might help, but it does not completely resolve the issue." He also noted that such an agreement "still brings uncertainty, leaving questions about what will happen after 60 days." Bailey further mentioned that he has not entirely dismissed a scenario where energy prices fall rapidly without affecting core inflation. However, for this scenario to prompt the central bank to cut rates, "you must have greater confidence" that the shock will not last long.

data.title: Bitcoin Depot Faces Class Action Over $76,000 ATM Scam
data.description: Karen and Robert Lacey have initiated a federal class action lawsuit against Bitcoin Depot following a $76,000 scam involving Bitcoin ATMs. According to NS3.AI, the complaint alleges that scammers impersonated Norton customer service representatives and FBI agents to defraud the plaintiffs. Bitcoin Depot, which filed for voluntary Chapter 11 bankruptcy on May 18, 2026, has since closed over 9,000 ATMs across North America.

data.title: Bitcoin Surpasses $74,000 Amid US-Iran Ceasefire Hopes
data.description: Bitcoin (BTC) rose above $74,000 on Friday as traders reacted to potential progress in US-Iran ceasefire talks, despite conflicting statements from both sides. According to BeInCrypto, BTC traded near $74,161, up 1.1% over 24 hours, following President Donald Trump's indication of a draft framework. Trump stated Iran must abandon nuclear weapons and allow toll-free passage through the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran demands $12 billion in frozen assets and no toll-free clause. The differing accounts have kept a final agreement elusive, impacting market sentiment.

data.title: Dell's Record Q1 Earnings Propel AI Infrastructure Rally
data.description: Dell Technologies surged 28.54% after reporting record Q1 earnings and raising its FY27 guidance, sparking a rally in AI infrastructure stocks. According to BeInCrypto, Dell's results confirmed the accelerating AI capital expenditure trend, with $24.4 billion in AI orders booked during the quarter. This led to gains for Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Super Micro Computer, Oracle, Microsoft, and Micron Technology. Meanwhile, oil prices retreated as Donald Trump hinted at a potential US-Iran deal, which could reopen the Strait of Hormuz, easing cost pressures across cyclical sectors.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Lenz Research Study Reveals Disagreement Among AI Models on Fact-Check Claims
data.description: A recent study by Lenz Research has highlighted significant discrepancies among five frontier AI models when evaluating real-world fact-check claims. According to NS3.AI, the study found that these AI models disagreed on 67% of the 1,000 claims analyzed. The research reported a Krippendorff's alpha of 0.639, which falls below the 0.8 reliability threshold mentioned in the study, indicating a lack of consensus among the models.

data.title: Fed's Daly Emphasizes Importance of Price Stability Without Economic Harm
data.description: Restoring price stability is crucial, but it should not come at the expense of the economy, according to Mary C. Daly, President of the San Francisco Fed. According to ChainCatcher, Daly highlighted the need to balance efforts to stabilize prices with maintaining economic health.

data.title: Alex Mashinsky Seeks to Overturn 12-Year Sentence for Fraud
data.description: Alex Mashinsky has filed a motion in a New York court to overturn his 12-year sentence for fraud and market manipulation. According to NS3.AI, Mashinsky submitted the motion without additional legal representation, following his announcement on May 5 that he would represent himself in the proceedings.

data.title: Crypto Policy Highlights: U.S. Regulation, Bermuda Blockchain, and European Banks
data.description: A recent analysis highlights five significant themes in the crypto policy landscape, focusing on developments in U.S. regulation, Bermuda's blockchain adoption, and European banking activities under the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework. According to NS3.AI, the discussion around the CLARITY Act, concerns over the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's (CFTC) credibility, and Hester Peirce's anticipated departure from the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) are pivotal in shaping U.S. regulatory approaches. Additionally, Bermuda's proactive policy efforts to integrate blockchain technology and Banca Sella's expansion of crypto services in Europe are seen as key advancements in the global crypto ecosystem.

data.title: Fed's Daly: AI Unlikely to Cause Mass Unemployment
data.description: Mary C. Daly, President of the San Francisco Federal Reserve, stated that large-scale unemployment or job losses are not expected due to artificial intelligence. According to Jin10, Daly's comments suggest a more optimistic outlook on the impact of AI on the labor market.

data.title: SEC Charges Texas Resident for Fraudulent AI Crypto Trading Scheme
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has filed a lawsuit against Nathan Fuller, a resident of Texas, accusing him of illegally raising approximately $12.3 million from around 150 investors through a fraudulent 'AI crypto trading robot' scheme. 

According to Odaily, the SEC stated that from October 2022 to mid-2024, Fuller sold crypto investment interests under the names Privvy Investments LLC and Gateway Digital Investments. He claimed to use an 'AI high-frequency arbitrage robot' for trading crypto assets, promising investors guaranteed returns of 40% to over 100% within 21 to 45 days.

The regulatory body alleged that Fuller falsely claimed that investment funds were protected by FDIC insurance, surety bonds, and professional liability insurance. In reality, the purported trading robot did not operate as advertised. The SEC accused Fuller of misappropriating at least $6.2 million of investor funds for personal expenses and using approximately $5.5 million from new investors for 'Ponzi-style payments.' He also allegedly misled investors by fabricating account statements and creating fictitious institutional communications.

The SEC has filed the lawsuit in the federal court for the Southern District of Texas, charging Fuller with violations related to securities issuance and anti-fraud laws. The commission is seeking a permanent injunction, disgorgement of ill-gotten gains, and civil penalties.

data.title: ExxonMobil VP Warns of Imminent Oil Price Surge
data.description: ExxonMobil's senior vice president, Neil Chapman, has warned that global oil inventory tightness could reach critical levels within weeks, potentially driving Brent crude prices to $150 or $160 per barrel. According to BeInCrypto, Chapman highlighted at a Bernstein investor conference that the market is nearing rarely seen stockpile levels, exacerbated by disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. The International Energy Agency reported a drawdown of 246 million barrels in March and April, with cumulative supply losses possibly exceeding one billion barrels by month-end. Analysts suggest commercial inventories are weaker than data indicates, raising concerns about potential energy shortages.

data.title: U.S. Money Market Funds Reach Record $8.281 Trillion Amid Fed Uncertainty
data.description: Investors have increased the total size of U.S. money market funds to a record $8.281 trillion due to uncertainty surrounding the Federal Reserve's monetary policy. According to Odaily, Crane Data's latest figures show that approximately $66 billion flowed into the money market fund industry in the week ending May 28. Of this, around $41 billion was added on Thursday as investors adjusted their portfolios before the month's end. Since the beginning of the year, money market funds have attracted about $172 billion.

data.title: HypeStrat's Holdings Surpass $1 Billion as BitMine Faces $8 Billion Loss
data.description: HypeStrat, a cryptocurrency treasury company, has seen its holdings exceed $1 billion in unrealized gains as HYPE continues to reach new all-time highs. According to Odaily, this positions HypeStrat as the top-performing crypto treasury company. In contrast, Ethereum treasury company BitMine (BMNR) is experiencing significant losses, with unrealized losses exceeding $8 billion, marking the largest loss among all crypto treasury companies.

data.title: Wintermute Provides Liquidity for Prediction Markets as CFTC Approves Kalshi's Bitcoin Futures
data.description: Wintermute has confirmed its role in providing two-sided liquidity for event contracts on major prediction-market platforms. According to NS3.AI, the London-based firm handles over $3.5 trillion in trading volume annually. Sources indicate that the platforms include Polymarket and Kalshi. In a related development, the CFTC has issued an order permitting Kalshi to offer U.S. perpetual futures linked to Bitcoin's price.
